steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify scale factor and apply the area scaling rule
steps.content: Each side of EFGH is 6 times the corresponding side of ABCD, giving a linear scale factor of $k = 6$. For similar figures, areas scale by $k^2$, so the area scale factor is $6^2 = 36$.

steps.title: Calculate the area of EFGH
steps.content: $A_{\text{EFGH}} = 66 \times 36 = 2,376$ square units. That confirms D is correct.
